mnemonics: Interior is INside, open and free. Closure is the CLOSEd set, including the boundary.
keyPoints: The interior of a set A is the largest open set contained within A.
The closure of a set A is the smallest closed set that contains A.
A point is in the interior if there exists an open ball around it entirely in A. A point is in the closure if every open ball around it intersects A.
commonMistakes: Assuming the interior of a set is always non-empty; a set like a single point in R has an empty interior.
Confusing a limit point for an interior point; a limit point only requires every neighborhood to contain *some* point of A, not necessarily the center point itself.
Thinking the closure is simply the set plus its limit points; while often true, the formal definition is the intersection of all closed sets containing A.
